strengths: Demonstrated proper needle loading technique with consistent driving angle
Successfully completed multiple suture passes through the Penrose drain
Maintained good visualization and camera positioning throughout the procedure
weaknesses: Failed to complete the task within the 10-minute time limit
Struggled with knot tying efficiency and sequence completion
Showed hesitation and rework during the suturing process

drills.name: Timed knot completion drill
drills.description: Practice completing the full three-throw sequence (surgeon's knot plus two single throws) within 2 minutes after suture placement
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the task within time limit

drills.name: Double-then-single sequence drill
drills.description: Rehearse one surgeon's knot followed by two single throws without time pressure to build muscle memory

drills.name: Economy-of-motion ladder
drills.description: Perform the complete task slowly with minimal wasted regrasping and instrument repositioning
drills.targets_weakness: Showed hesitation and rework during suturing

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your solid technical foundation into faster, more decisive execution. Your needle driving is competent, but knot tying efficiency needs targeted practice to achieve consistent task completion within the time limit.
